title: consolidate developer docs in gen; enter only emits its own schema
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Enter's `docs.ts` shrinks from 1460 to 29 lines. All schema decoration — info description, tags, alias filtering, media merge, x-codeSamples, response examples — moves to gen and runs as a post-merge transform. Enter now exposes just two routes: a 301 redirect to gen for `/docs/`, and the raw schema endpoint gen consumes via the `ENTER` service binding.

Why:
- Gen owns the public docs surface, so all decoration belongs there.
- Before: alias lists drifted (enter only filtered image/text, gen also covered audio/embedding); media schema was fetched twice per docs render; ~12 of 17 `CODE_SAMPLES` entries silently no-op'd because they targeted gen-owned paths that enter doesn't have.
- After: enter mirrors media — raw schema in, gen merges and decorates. Single mental model.

Also normalizes two route tags from `"Account"` to `"👤 Account"` ([api-keys.ts:377](enter.pollinations.ai/src/routes/api-keys.ts#L377), [app-lookup.ts:68](enter.pollinations.ai/src/routes/app-lookup.ts#L68)) so they group correctly in Scalar.

Verified: enter biome + tsc + `test/docs-redirect.test.ts` (3/3), gen biome + tsc + `test/docs.test.ts` (6/6).

Fixes #11296

gist.headline: One source of truth
gist.blurb: We've reorganized how our API documentation is generated under the hood. The result? A cleaner, more accurate reference page with all the code samples actually working.
gist.summary: Moved API schema decoration—including tags, alias filtering, and code samples—from the enter service to gen, reducing enter's docs route from 1460 to 29 lines.
gist.impact: The API reference now correctly displays all 17 code samples and properly groups the '👤 Account' routes in Scalar.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a librarian, organizing scattered documents into one central, glowing folder or book. Nomnom eating the old duplicate papers.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, organizing glowing digital scrolls into a single neat filing cabinet. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats a messy stack of old duplicate papers nearby. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
